title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Hang, I feel like I'm going to learn so much today.
But they are just thinking, we're going to use this area for something else.
It depends on who you are and what you're going to do because obviously it's a massive privilege.

title: will / shall
examples: They'll say, like, I was born in such-and-such province in China.
And increasing tensions and increasing fears on the peninsula will have an effect on any election.
Students will go abroad.
They'll send athletes to athletic competitions.
Personally, I think based on the kind of patriarchal system that they have, it will be one of his sons.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Can you hear the difference between a North Korean person speaking, the South Korean person speaking?
But for the most part, like anything I've read from North Korea, I've been able to read pretty easily.
One thing I've noticed a lot of people are shocked by is North Koreans can leave North Korea.
A lot of people think like, well, they can't leave.
I'm not saying that it's easy and anyone can do it.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: Is there anything else that you think of the general population might not know about North Korea?

title: have to / must
examples: I did not anticipate that they would keep it up for as many months as they have to cast it out.

title: should (advice)
examples: I'm of the opinion that everyone should be able to decide what is ethical or moral for themselves.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: First, I would like to introduce today's lovely guest.
I did not anticipate that they would keep it up for as many months as they have to cast it out.
Do you think you would ever visit North Korea if you had the chance?
So I personally have not decided yet if I would ever go.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: But if you go to either the JSA or the DMZ but very close to the JSA, there is a train station there.
And another thing to keep in mind is, if you do a DMZ tour, you're most likely have a South Korean guide.
Do you think you would ever visit North Korea if you had the chance?

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: Interestingly, North Korean has a few letters that are different from Hangul in South Korea.
They'll say, like, I was born in such-and-such province in China.
All of this was built.
One thing I've noticed a lot of people are shocked by is North Koreans can leave North Korea.
